message:
perf(sync): full-scan export hydration + skip-probe import path

Two related changes to src/sync/mod.rs that both lean on the new
storage helpers introduced in the previous commit.

Export side — switch from per-batch hydration to full-scan when large
---------------------------------------------------------------------
- Add `EXPORT_FULL_SCAN_MIN_ISSUES = 512`. When the export ID list is
  >= this threshold, route through the new `hydrate_export_issues_full_scan`
  helper, which calls `storage.get_all_issues_for_export()` once,
  retains rows whose id is in the export set, and bulk-loads
  dependencies / labels / comments via the existing
  `get_all_dependency_records`, `get_all_labels`,
  `get_all_comments` accessors.
- Below the threshold, the existing `hydrate_export_issue_batch`
  per-chunk path still runs — it remains better for small exports
  because the relation queries fall back to per-id fetches when the
  bulk loader fails.
- The new `hydrate_export_issues` dispatcher is the single entry point;
  `export_to_jsonl_with_policy` and `export_to_writer_with_policy` no
  longer iterate `chunks(EXPORT_ISSUE_BATCH_SIZE)` themselves — the
  batching decision is now owned by the hydrator.
- Errors raised while building the bulk maps are surfaced through the
  same `ExportContext::handle_error` path so error policy semantics
  (`RequiredCore` vs everything else) are unchanged.

Import side — skip the existence probe on `CollisionAction::Insert`
-------------------------------------------------------------------
- New `insert_new_import_issue` wraps `storage.insert_new_issue_for_import`
  (which goes straight to INSERT, no probe). On a fresh import this
  saves one SELECT per issue.
- If a primary-key or unique-violation comes back — meaning a
  concurrent writer beat us in or the upstream caller misclassified the
  action — we log at debug and fall back to `upsert_issue_for_import`,
  which preserves the prior semantics. Other database errors still
  propagate.
- `process_import_action` only swaps the `CollisionAction::Insert` arm;
  the `Update` and `Skip` arms are untouched.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
